questions.question:
Consider the following logical inferences. I1: If it rains then the cricket match will not be played.
The cricket match was played.
Inference: There was no rain.
I2: If it rains then the cricket match will not be played.
It did not rain.
Inference: The cricket match was played.

Which of the following is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2012)
options:
A.

Both I1 and I2 are correct inferences

B.

I1 is correct but I2 is not a correct inference

C.

I1 is not correct but I2 is a correct inference

D.

Both I1 and I2 are not correct inferences
questions.correct_answer:
B.

I1 is correct but I2 is not a correct inference
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
A deck of 5 cards (each carrying a distinct number from 1 to 5) is shuffled thoroughly. Two cards are then removed one at a time from the deck. What is the probability that the two cards are selected with the number on the first card being one higher than the number on the second card?

(GATE CSE 2011)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider a finite sequence of random values \(X=[x_{1},x_{2},...,x_{n}]\). Let \(\mu _{x}\) be the mean and \(\sigma _{x}\) be the standard deviation of X .Let another finite sequence Y of equal length be derived from this as \(y_{i}=a*x_{i}+b\), where a and b are positive constants. Let Let \(\mu _{y}\) be the mean and \(\sigma _{y}\) be the standard deviation of this sequence. Which one of the following statements is INCORRECT?

(GATE CSE 2011)
options:
A.

Index position of mode of X in X is the same as the index position of mode of Y in Y.

B.

Index position of median of X in X is the same as the index position of median of Y in Y.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which one of the following options is CORRECT given three positive integers x, y and z, and a predicate
\(P(x)=\neg (x=1)\wedge \forall y(\exists z(x=y*z))\Rightarrow (y=x)\vee (y=1)\)

(GATE CSE 2011)
options:
A.

P(x) being true means that x is a prime number

B.

P(x) being true means that x is a number other than 1

C.

P(x) is always true irrespective of the value of x

D.

P(x) being true means that x has exactly two factors other than 1 and x
questions.correct_answer:
A.

P(x) being true means that x is a prime number
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Suppose the predicate F(x,y,t) is used to represent the statement that person x can fool person y at time t. which one of the statements below expresses best the meaning of the formula \(\forall x \exists y \exists t(\neg F (x, y, t))\)?

(GATE CSE 2010)
options:
A.

Everyone can fool some person at some time

B.

No one can fool everyone all the time

C.

Everyone cannot fool some person all the time

D.

No one can fool some person at some time
questions.correct_answer:
B.

No one can fool everyone all the time
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:

I. 7, 6, 5, 4, 4, 3, 2, 1
II. 6, 6, 6, 6, 3, 3, 2, 2
III. 7, 6, 6, 4, 4, 3, 2, 2
IV. 8, 7, 7, 6, 4, 2, 1, 1

(GATE CSE 2010)
